summary: The Fairy Tales 2 students were all motivated. They could all do the exercises well enough, make sentences with a comparative, understand and hear the mistakes in the songs, and read very well. Hugo was sometimes making a couple of mistakes with the pronunciation. He could complete the gaps during one of the listening exercises.

The group went well overall: they were all polite, motivated, and interested. They could remember parts of the story quite well, answer using full structures, understand the vocabulary well, and do the games well too. Hugo struggled a bit with pronunciation, but could repeat the correct words. In the My Talent Body 1 part, he made spelling mistakes like “wich” instead of “which”.

Hugo was happy to be there and took part in the activities enthusiastically. He loved the Dice Game, drawing and explaining expressions. He still made a few mistakes orally, but he is always willing to correct himself. He could tell me riddles, did his exercises seriously, and reviewed the different articles in texts. He was fun to teach thanks to his motivation. He is always comfortable with being corrected and finding the correct answers.

Hugo was attentive and motivated as always. Hugo and Nina had forgotten how to use connectors such as although and despite. Hugo still makes some pronunciation mistakes and sometimes makes tense mistakes like “didn’t found.” The class could do the exercises quite well and understand the new vocabulary very well too, and they all did an effort to express themselves.

They could all express themselves rather well when talking about food groups, dishes and meals. They could use the structures given by the teacher very well, and they worked on an argumentative text quite well. Hugo made a couple of grammatical mistakes, like using a verb in -ing after could, and he did some spelling mistakes, but he could correct them almost all directly when they were pointed out to him. Hugo and Clara could talk about obligations and prohibitions very well when talking about primary and secondary school rules. Hugo sometimes needs a bit of help with pronunciation. Nina and Hugo needed a bit of help for inspiration, and Hugo asked me the purpose of the connector before using it.

Hugo was calm and attentive. He did the work he was asked and did it as well as he could. He could read the chapter quite well, without pronunciation mistakes, but he was not totally fluent. He could summarize the chapters using the target language written on the board rather well, make questions from the target language, answer using it too, and understand a song and answer questions about it. He could form a question and an answer using “would rather…than…” and “prefer… to…”. He could not really use vocabulary words and make sentences with them that showed understanding of the words.

Hugo could answer questions using the target language quite well. They could do the listening activity quite well and could report what the characters had said quite well, although I needed to tell Hugo to mind the ‘s’ at the 3rd person singular. Clara and Hugo struggled a bit with the grammar revision, but still enjoyed the game. Their game revision of the vocabulary was better. Hugo’s pronunciation was alright and he always takes my remarks into account. He also helped Nina spell 'teen' for her My Talent task.

Hugo could do the reading exercise and fill in the blanks with the listening exercise very, very well, and he guessed almost all of the words. That was great. He also did well in the oral exercises, and even though he still sometimes makes mistakes, he can almost always correct himself, and sometimes he realizes it and corrects them directly without needing my help. He had a harder time guessing the meaning of some words from my definitions, matching them.

Hugo took part in the activities quite well and could use the target language in the gapped sentences quite well. He still makes some mistakes when he speaks, but he always tries to correct himself, and when I point out what he needs to correct, he does it. They could do the grammar activity very well and seemed to have fun doing the games too.

Hugo only made a few mistakes, very few mistakes, when he had to express himself orally. He showed quite a good knowledge of the grammatical structures we've seen so far and could talk about the story and the previous chapters quite well, using some of the target language.

Hugo was very motivated. He made some mistakes, usually the same ones: he forgets to use the s at the end of verbs when he talks in the third person singular, and he sometimes makes mistakes with articles, but he can always correct himself. He can also speak fluently about topics like music, and when he doesn't understand something, he can ask for clarification. He was in a lovely mood, and it was lovely to have him in class.
